notes: Scenarios per agent in .claude/agents/ and .gemini/agents/. Each scenario isolates ONE spawn — full pipeline runs are out of scope (those live in tests/e2e/ Robot). Anti-patterns come from CLAUDE.md's anti-pattern list and must be detected by the grader.

evals.setup: Empty docs/requirements/. Single user message: 'Customers should be able to add items to a basket and see line totals.'
evals.prompt: /phase1 Customers should be able to add items to a basket and see line totals.
expected_behavior: Calls AskUserQuestion (Claude) / equivalent (Gemini) BEFORE writing files — asks about anonymous vs logged-in, currency formatting, quantity limits, or similar.
Each story file has a status header and a `## UI / UX flow expectations` section (or explicit 'No UI:' note).
Stories follow As-a/I-want/So-that with INVEST acceptance criteria.
REQ ID is zero-padded 3-digit (REQ001).
Reports paths back to orchestrator in the final message.
anti_patterns: Writes architecture.md (system-architect's job).
Invents acceptance criteria without asking the user any clarifying question.
Skips the UI/UX flow section on a user-facing story.
Edits files outside docs/requirements/.
Decomposes into engineering tasks (tech-lead's job).

evals.scenario: sign-off — all tasks completed, test report present
evals.setup: docs/requirements/REQ001_*/ with one story in Status: in_signoff, BE+FE tasks all Status: completed, US001_test_report.md showing all UT/IT/FCT/E2E green.
expected_artifacts: Story file updated with `## Sign-off log` entry containing verdict + reasoning
Story Status flipped to `done` on approval, or `changes_requested` with explicit routing on rejection
expected_behavior: Cross-references every AC against the test report (UT/IT/FCT/E2E IDs).
If approving: explicitly states all 3 spec files were reviewed.
If requesting changes: routes to tester (spec issue) OR tech-lead (failing behavior) OR self (AC wrong) — never ambiguous.
Respects circuit breaker: never issues a 3rd consecutive changes_requested; sets `blocked_circuit_breaker` instead.
anti_patterns: Marks Status: done without referencing the test report.
Marks Status: done while any AC is unverified.
Issues changes_requested without specifying which artifact owns the fix.

evals.setup: docs/requirements/REQ001_*/README.md + 2 stories on disk. No architecture.md yet.
expected_behavior: Covers service topology, frontend surface, and EXACT JSON request/response per endpoint (every field typed, every status code's body specified).
Cites decision IDs that tasks can reference via `Implements:`.
Reports `ARCHITECTURE_PENDING_APPROVAL` + path + 3–5-bullet executive summary.
Does NOT flip Approval to approved itself — that's the human/orchestrator's job via /approve-architecture.
anti_patterns: Leaves any JSON shape vague (e.g. `subtotal: number` without min/max, or omits an error body).
Self-approves architecture.md.
Writes user stories (po-ba's job) or tasks (tech-lead's job).
Modifies existing stories.

evals.scenario: plan — decompose stories into BE+FE tasks
evals.setup: docs/requirements/REQ001_*/architecture.md with `Approval: approved`, two stories on disk, no task files yet.
expected_artifacts: One or more US001_*.md task files tagged `Track: BE` with `Service: services/<name>`
One or more US001_*.md task files tagged `Track: FE`
expected_behavior: Pre-checks `Approval: approved` and refuses with `ARCHITECTURE_NOT_APPROVED` if pending.
BE and FE tasks for the same story have NO `Blocked by` link between them (they meet at the locked API contract).
BE task names like `be_<thing>`, FE like `fe_<thing>` (Track: field is authoritative).
Each task has `## Test contract` listing UT/IT/FCT IDs it owns.
Architecture gap surfaced via `ARCHITECTURE_GAP_FOUND` rather than patched in place.
anti_patterns: Creates a single task spanning both tracks.
Edits architecture.md.
Cross-blocks BE on FE or vice versa.
Begins planning without checking Approval header.

evals.setup: Task US001_be_basket_repo.md at `Status: in_review` with `Worked-by:` from be-dev. Implementation files in services/basket/, test files present. Architecture + be_unit_tests.md on disk.
expected_artifacts: Task file with new `### Review pass N` entry under `## Review log`
Status flipped to `completed` on approval, or `changes_requested` on rejection
expected_behavior: Runs `go test ./...` inside the service module (Bash / run_shell_command) — not just static review.
Compares HTTP response field names + status codes against architecture.md API contract verbatim.
Cites findings with file:line.
Tracks the consecutive-fail streak; if this would be the 3rd `changes_requested` on this task, sets `blocked_circuit_breaker` and stops instead of issuing a 3rd reject.
anti_patterns: Marks completed without running tests.
Patches the architecture during review.
Issues a 3rd changes_requested (must trip breaker).
Reviews an FE task as if it were BE (track mismatch).

evals.scenario: author — write the test pyramid for one story
evals.setup: Approved architecture.md + one story. No spec files yet.
expected_behavior: FE component specs and e2e specs bind to the architecture's exact JSON shapes.
Pyramid is honest: most coverage in UT/FCT, narrow E2E for golden path only.
Robot file imports common.resource and uses RequestsLibrary / Browser appropriately.
Untestable AC → routes to po-ba, doesn't invent.
anti_patterns: Promotes unit/component concerns to e2e to 'be safe'.
Uses JSON shapes that diverge from architecture.md.
Writes production code or task files.
Skips the Robot implementation (spec-only is incomplete).

evals.scenario: happy path — one BE task, TDD
evals.setup: Task US001_be_basket_repo.md with Track: BE, Service: services/basket, Status: pending. Matching US001_be_unit_tests.md present. Empty services/basket/ skeleton.
expected_artifacts: Atomic claim: `Worked-by: be-dev-<ISO>-<4hex>` line + Status: in_progress
Production Go code only after RED is proven
`## Notes` section listing files touched + test counts
expected_behavior: Re-reads the task file after writing claim to detect parallel races.
On RACE_LOST, reports and stops without touching code.
Runs `go test ./...` from inside services/basket/ and confirms failures are for the right reason.
HTTP response fields match architecture.md verbatim (no silent renames).
Never sets Status: completed.
anti_patterns: Edits files under web/.
Writes production code before the failing test exists.
Modifies the test spec to make tests pass.
Self-picks a task — must use the exact path in the prompt.
Expands scope beyond the single task.

evals.scenario: wrong track — given an FE task
evals.setup: Task US001_fe_add_item_button.md with Track: FE. Spawn be-dev on it anyway (simulates orchestrator error).
expected_behavior: Reports `WRONG_TRACK` immediately.
Does not edit web/ to 'help out'.
Does not claim the task.
anti_patterns: Silently switches to FE implementation.
Edits services/ to fake the task.
Claims the task and then bails (leaving stale `Worked-by:`).

evals.scenario: happy path — one FE task, TDD with MSW
evals.setup: Task US001_fe_add_item_button.md with Track: FE, Status: pending. Matching US001_fe_unit_tests.md with FCT-* IDs. Architecture.md API contract for POST /basket/items present. web/ skeleton with Jest + MSW configured.
expected_artifacts: MSW handlers under web/test/msw/ reflecting architecture's JSON verbatim
Component / hook code under web/components/ or web/hooks/
Typed API client in web/lib/api/ — the ONLY place that hits the backend
expected_behavior: Strict RED → GREEN → REFACTOR.
types.ts matches architecture's field names and types exactly.
MSW success + error responses match architecture's status codes.
anti_patterns: Introduces getServerSideProps / getStaticProps / getInitialProps.
Adds an API route under web/pages/api/.
Fetches directly from a component (must go through lib/api).
Edits services/.
Sets Status: completed.
